queries.question: Give me a list of magazine names and publishers along with the associated demographic groups and professional fields
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    d.demographic_group, professional_field,
    m.name AS magazine_name,
    m.publisher
FROM 
    Demographics d
JOIN 
    Magazines m ON d.magazineID = m.magazineID;
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?demographic_group ?professional_field ?name ?publisher WHERE {
  ?d a :Demographics .
  ?m a :Magazines .
  ?d p:hasMagazines ?m .
  ?d p:demographic_group ?demographic_group .
  ?d p:professional_field ?professional_field .
  ?m p:name ?name .
  ?m p:publisher ?publisher .
}

queries.question: In each professional field, how many magazines cater for them.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    professional_field,
    COUNT(*) AS magazine_count
FROM 
    Demographics
GROUP BY 
    professional_field;
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?professional_field_g (COUNT(*) AS ?agg) WHERE {
  ?demographics a :Demographics .
  ?demographics p:professional_field ?professional_field_g .
}
GROUP BY ?professional_field_g

queries.question: For each demographic group , show how many magazines cater for them.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    demographic_group,
    COUNT(*) AS magazine_count
FROM 
    Demographics
GROUP BY 
    demographic_group;
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?demographic_group_g (COUNT(*) AS ?agg) WHERE {
  ?demographics a :Demographics .
  ?demographics p:demographic_group ?demographic_group_g .
}
GROUP BY ?demographic_group_g

queries.question: For each demographic group and professional field, show how many magazines cater for them.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    demographic_group, professional_field,
    COUNT(*) AS magazine_count
FROM 
    Demographics
GROUP BY 
    demographic_group, professional_field;
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/journalism/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?demographic_group_g ?professional_field_g (COUNT(*) AS ?agg) WHERE {
  ?demographics a :Demographics .
  ?demographics p:demographic_group ?demographic_group_g .
  ?demographics p:professional_field ?professional_field_g .
}
GROUP BY ?demographic_group_g ?professional_field_g

How many magazines cater to each audience demographic
